sections.heading: Implementation Blueprint
paragraphs: A Step-by-Step Rollout Plan for SMBs
Version 1.0  |  April 2026  |  Confidential – Internal Use Only
tables: Confident AI, Controlled Risk.   ·   govsecure.ai   ·   AI Governance Platform for SMBs

sections.heading: Executive Summary
paragraphs: This 90-Day AI Governance Implementation Blueprint provides Govsecure clients with a structured, actionable roadmap for standing up a complete AI governance program from scratch. Designed specifically for small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs), it transforms the comprehensive Govsecure AI Governance Checklist Templates into a phased sequence of concrete activities, ownership assignments, and measurable milestones.

At the end of 90 days, your organization will have a live AI inventory, a documented policy framework, completed risk assessments for active AI systems, trained staff, and a repeatable operational rhythm for ongoing AI governance—all aligned to NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, and emerging regulatory requirements.

sections.heading: What This Blueprint Delivers
tables: Every AI tool, model, and vendor feature documented and risk-tiered.
Foundational policies covering use, risk, privacy, and vendor management.
Completed risk assessments for all Active/High-risk AI use cases.
Documented assessments for all AI-enabled vendors and SaaS tools.
Reviewer roles, escalation paths, and override procedures in place.
AI-specific IR procedures tested and embedded in existing processes.
Role-based AI literacy and governance training completed across key staff.
Recurring review schedule established with assigned owners.

Govsecure Tip:  AI governance does not require a large team or a big budget. It requires clarity, consistency, and accountability. This blueprint is designed so that a single part-time program owner, with cross-functional support, can complete it on schedule.

sections.heading: How to Use This Blueprint
paragraphs: This blueprint is organized into three 30-day phases, each broken down by week. Every week includes a structured activity table referencing the specific Govsecure checklist template that drives the work. Use it alongside your Govsecure checklist files—not as a replacement for them.

sections.heading: Core Roles
tables: Owns the program, drives the blueprint, reports to leadership.
Submit use cases, confirm AI tools in their area, review risk tiers.
Provide technical context, conduct security reviews, manage integrations.
Review data handling, contracts, DPIA requirements.
Identify AI-enabled vendor agreements, support due diligence.
Approves policies, receives monthly status briefings.

sections.heading: Checklist Reference Key
paragraphs: Phase 1 lays the bedrock of your AI governance program. You will establish the governance structure, discover every AI system in use (including shadow AI), assign risk tiers, and launch foundational policy work. Nothing in the program can be calibrated without knowing what AI you actually have—so the AI inventory is the single most critical output of this phase.
tables: AI Third-Party / Vendor Due Diligence Checklist
AI Regulatory Change Impact Assessment Checklist

bullets: Stand up the AI Governance program with a named lead, executive sponsor, and cross-functional working group.
Complete a comprehensive AI Inventory covering all AI tools, models, vendors, and use cases.
Assign a risk tier (Low / Moderate / High / Critical) to every discovered AI use case.
Initiate foundational AI policy drafts (Acceptable Use, Risk, Data Handling).
Establish intake and submission processes for new AI use case requests.
Complete initial vendor identification for all AI-enabled third-party tools.
sections.heading: Phase 1 Objectives
tables: Appoint AI Governance Lead (AGL) and Executive Sponsor
Define program charter, scope, and 90-day success criteria
Set up program tracking system (Jira / SharePoint / GRC tool)
Schedule all Phase 1 recurring standups and checkpoints
Brief Executive Sponsor on program timeline and resource needs
Focus: AI Discovery & Shadow AI Detection
Focus: AI Discovery & Shadow AI Detection
Send AI discovery survey to all Business Unit Leads
Reconcile AI tools against procurement and vendor contract list
Reconcile AI tools against SSO / IdP app catalog
Reconcile AI tools against cloud / API inventory
Flag shadow AI tools discovered outside approved channels
Populate AI Inventory: identity, business context, and technical fields
Complete data and privacy fields for all discovered AI entries
Assign initial risk tier to every AI entry (Low/Moderate/High/Critical)
Flag any AI systems requiring DPIA / privacy impact assessment
Complete risk and compliance fields for all AI registry entries
Document applicable regulations per AI entry (CCPA, HIPAA, etc.)
Identify top 5 highest-risk AI use cases for priority assessment
Focus: AI Intake Process & Policy Kickoff
Focus: AI Intake Process & Policy Kickoff
Publish AI Inventory v1.0 to shared program repository
Design and launch AI Use Case Intake / Triage process
Define registry update cadence (monthly) and assign registry owner
Begin drafting AI Acceptable Use Policy
Begin drafting AI Risk Management Policy
Begin vendor identification list for AI Third-Party Due Diligence

sections.heading: Phase Milestones & Success Criteria
paragraphs: Phase 2 is where governance goes deep. You will complete risk assessments for priority AI systems, run security and privacy reviews, conduct vendor due diligence, finalize and approve foundational policies, and begin standing up human oversight and monitoring frameworks. This phase converts your inventory into an assessed, documented, and controlled AI portfolio.

bullets: Complete risk assessments for all High/Critical AI use cases; triage Moderate cases.
Finalize and gain executive approval for the AI Acceptable Use Policy and AI Risk Management Policy.
Complete vendor due diligence assessments for all active AI-enabled vendors.
Conduct security reviews for all production AI systems.
Complete privacy screening and flag DPIA candidates.
Design human oversight controls and escalation paths for high-risk AI decisions.
Stand up the AI Model Validation framework for any internally developed or fine-tuned models.
sections.heading: Phase 2 Objectives
tables: Focus: Risk Assessments – High & Critical AI Use Cases
Focus: Risk Assessments – High & Critical AI Use Cases
Conduct full risk assessment for each High/Critical AI use case
Document risk findings, controls, and residual risk per use case
Link risk assessment records to AI registry entries
Flag unacceptable residual risks for executive decision / suspension
Begin security review for production AI systems
Identify and document applicable compliance obligations per AI use case
Focus: Security Reviews & Vendor Due Diligence
Focus: Security Reviews & Vendor Due Diligence
Complete AI Security Review for all production AI systems
Document access controls, MFA/SSO coverage, API scopes per system
Identify and document logging / audit trail gaps
Send AI Third-Party Due Diligence Questionnaire to all AI vendors
Review vendor DPA / data processing agreements for AI-specific terms
Document vendor training/retention terms and cross-border data transfers
Focus: Privacy Screening, Policy Finalization & DPIA
Focus: Privacy Screening, Policy Finalization & DPIA
Complete Privacy & DPIA Screening for all flagged AI use cases
Initiate DPIAs for use cases that meet DPIA threshold criteria
Finalize AI Acceptable Use Policy – legal and exec review cycle
Finalize AI Risk Management Policy – legal and exec review cycle
Draft AI Data Handling & Privacy Policy
Review and finalize Vendor Due Diligence responses from vendors
Focus: Human Oversight, Model Validation & Policy Approval
Focus: Human Oversight, Model Validation & Policy Approval
Define reviewer roles and decision boundaries for all High/Critical AI
Document escalation paths, override rules, and fallback procedures
Confirm human oversight controls are operational for production AI
Complete Model Validation / Testing Checklist for internally developed models
Gain executive approval: Acceptable Use Policy
Gain executive approval: Risk Management Policy
Publish approved policies to internal policy repository

sections.heading: Phase Milestones & Success Criteria
paragraphs: Phase 3 converts your governance program from a project into a repeatable operational discipline. You will complete staff training, stand up monitoring and revalidation processes, finalize and test the AI incident response playbook, complete change management procedures, and deliver a 90-Day Program Completion Report to your executive leadership. Governance must run after Day 90—this phase ensures it does.
tables: VDD questionnaires sent to all AI vendors
PRI screening log; DPIA intake list
HOE checklist complete per use case
Two core policies approved by Exec

bullets: Complete role-based AI governance training for all staff categories.
Stand up the AI Monitoring & Revalidation process with assigned owners and schedules.
Finalize and tabletop-test the AI Incident Response Playbook.
Document and activate Change Management procedures for AI system updates.
Complete regulatory change mapping for applicable AI laws and frameworks.
Deliver the 90-Day AI Governance Completion Report to leadership.
Establish the ongoing governance calendar (quarterly reviews, annual policy refresh).
sections.heading: Phase 3 Objectives
tables: Deploy role-based AI governance training for Business Users
Deploy AI governance training for IT / Developers / Data Teams
Brief Executive Team on AI governance program status and obligations
Track training completion rates per role category
Draft AI Incident Response Playbook (AI-specific IR procedures)
Identify AI incident categories and response owners
Assign monitoring owners for every Active AI use case
Define KPIs and KRIs for each monitored AI system
Set up monitoring schedules (continuous / quarterly / semi-annual by tier)
Configure logging and alerting for High/Critical AI systems
Finalize AI Change Management Checklist and process
Document what constitutes a 'material change' requiring re-review
Focus: Incident Response Testing & Regulatory Mapping
Focus: Incident Response Testing & Regulatory Mapping
Tabletop test AI Incident Response Playbook (hallucination scenario)
Tabletop test AI Incident Response Playbook (data leakage scenario)
Document IR test findings and update playbook accordingly
Complete Regulatory Change Impact Assessment for current AI regulations
Map applicable regulations to specific AI use cases and required controls
Confirm training completion at or above 90% target for all roles
Complete evidence pack for all High/Critical AI use cases
Conduct final registry quality check – all fields complete and current
Establish ongoing AI governance calendar (quarterly reviews, annual refresh)
Present 90-Day Completion Report to Executive Leadership
Transition program to steady-state operational ownership

sections.heading: Phase Milestones & Success Criteria
tables: Role-based training deployed to all staff categories
Monitoring owners and schedules assigned – all AI
MON checklist per active use case
Evidence packs complete – High/Critical use cases

sections.heading: Program Health Metrics & KPIs
paragraphs: Track the following metrics throughout the 90 days to measure program health and report progress to your Executive Sponsor. These metrics align directly to the Govsecure AI Governance Checklist completion criteria.

sections.heading: Quick Reference: 90-Day Governance Calendar
tables: Working group formed, program charter, tracking system live
AI discovery complete, inventory v0.1, shadow AI flagged
Risk tiers assigned, privacy flags set, compliance obligations documented
Inventory v1.0 published, intake process live, policy drafts started
Risk assessments complete (High/Critical), security reviews initiated
Security reviews complete, VDD questionnaires sent
Privacy screening done, DPIAs initiated, policy final review
Human oversight documented, model validation done, 2 policies approved
All staff trained, IR playbook drafted
Monitoring owners + schedules active, change management live
IR playbook tabletop tested, regulatory mapping complete
Evidence packs done, 90-Day report presented, governance calendar set

Post-90 Days:  Run your first quarterly AI governance review (Month 6), annual policy refresh (Month 12), and use the Govsecure AI Monitoring & Revalidation Checklist for each scheduled revalidation cycle. Governance is not a project—it is an operating discipline.

sections.heading: About Govsecure
paragraphs: Govsecure is the AI Governance platform built for SMBs. Our checklist templates, intake forms, risk assessment frameworks, and implementation blueprints are designed to give small and mid-sized organizations enterprise-grade AI governance capabilities—without the enterprise-grade overhead. For questions, customization support, or platform access, contact your Govsecure program advisor.
